***Guardians of the Galaxy*** (retroactively referred to as ***Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 1***)[[4]](#cite_note-Vol1Title-4)[[5]](#cite_note-Vol1Title2-5) is a 2014 American [superhero film](/wiki/Superhero_film "Superhero film") based on the [Marvel Comics](/wiki/Marvel_Comics "Marvel Comics") superhero team [of the same name](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(2008_team) "Guardians of the Galaxy (2008 team)"). Produced by [Marvel Studios](/wiki/Marvel_Studios "Marvel Studios") and distributed by [Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures](/wiki/Walt_Disney_Studios_Motion_Pictures "Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures"), it is [the 10th film](/wiki/List_of_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe_films "List of Marvel Cinematic Universe films") in the [Marvel Cinematic Universe](/wiki/Marvel_Cinematic_Universe "Marvel Cinematic Universe") (MCU). Directed by [James Gunn](/wiki/James_Gunn "James Gunn"), who wrote the screenplay with [Nicole Perlman](/wiki/Nicole_Perlman "Nicole Perlman"), it features an [ensemble cast](/wiki/Ensemble_cast "Ensemble cast") including [Chris Pratt](/wiki/Chris_Pratt "Chris Pratt"), [Zoe Saldaña](/wiki/Zoe_Salda%C3%B1a "Zoe Saldaña"), [Dave Bautista](/wiki/Dave_Bautista "Dave Bautista"), [Vin Diesel](/wiki/Vin_Diesel "Vin Diesel"), and [Bradley Cooper](/wiki/Bradley_Cooper "Bradley Cooper") as the titular [Guardians](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Guardians of the Galaxy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), along with [Lee Pace](/wiki/Lee_Pace "Lee Pace"), [Michael Rooker](/wiki/Michael_Rooker "Michael Rooker"), [Karen Gillan](/wiki/Karen_Gillan "Karen Gillan"), [Djimon Hounsou](/wiki/Djimon_Hounsou "Djimon Hounsou"), [John C. Reilly](/wiki/John_C._Reilly "John C. Reilly"), [Glenn Close](/wiki/Glenn_Close "Glenn Close"), and [Benicio del Toro](/wiki/Benicio_del_Toro "Benicio del Toro"). In the film, [Peter Quill](/wiki/Peter_Quill_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") (Pratt) and a group of extraterrestrial criminals go on the run after stealing a [powerful artifact](/wiki/Power_Stone_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Power Stone (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

Perlman began working on the screenplay in 2009. Producer [Kevin Feige](/wiki/Kevin_Feige "Kevin Feige") first publicly mentioned *Guardians of the Galaxy* as a potential film in 2010 and Marvel Studios announced it was in active development at the July 2012 [San Diego Comic-Con](/wiki/San_Diego_Comic-Con "San Diego Comic-Con"). Gunn was hired to write and direct the film that September. In February 2013, Pratt was hired to play Peter Quill / Star-Lord, and the supporting cast members were subsequently confirmed. [Principal photography](/wiki/Principal_photography "Principal photography") began in July 2013 at [Shepperton Studios](/wiki/Shepperton_Studios "Shepperton Studios") in England, with filming continuing in London before wrapping in October 2013. In addition to an original score by [Tyler Bates](/wiki/Tyler_Bates "Tyler Bates"), the soundtrack includes several popular songs from the 1960s and 1970s chosen by Gunn. Post-production was completed on July 7, 2014.

*Guardians of the Galaxy* premiered at the [Dolby Theatre](/wiki/Dolby_Theatre "Dolby Theatre") in [Hollywood, Los Angeles](/wiki/Hollywood,_Los_Angeles "Hollywood, Los Angeles"), on July 21, 2014, and was theatrically released in the United States on August 1 as part of [Phase Two](/wiki/Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_Phase_Two "Marvel Cinematic Universe: Phase Two") of the MCU. It was a critical and commercial success, grossing $773.3 million worldwide and became the [third-highest-grossing film of 2014](/wiki/2014_in_film#Highest-grossing_films "2014 in film"). It was praised for its screenplay, direction, acting, humor, soundtrack, visual effects and action sequences. It was nominated for two awards at the [87th Academy Awards](/wiki/87th_Academy_Awards "87th Academy Awards"), and received [numerous other accolades](/wiki/List_of_accolades_received_by_Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(film) "List of accolades received by Guardians of the Galaxy (film)"). Two sequels have been released: *[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2 "Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2")* (2017) and *[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._3 "Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3")* (2023).

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(film)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

In 1988, following his mother's death, the child [Peter Quill](/wiki/Peter_Quill_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") is [abducted](/wiki/Alien_abduction "Alien abduction") from Earth by the [Ravagers](/wiki/Ravagers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ravagers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), a group of alien thieves and smugglers led by [Yondu Udonta](/wiki/Yondu_Udonta_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Yondu Udonta (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). In 2014, on the abandoned planet [Morag](/wiki/Morag_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Morag (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), Quill steals a mysterious Orb but is attacked by henchmen of the fanatical [Kree](/wiki/Kree_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Kree (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") renegade [Ronan the Accuser](/wiki/Ronan_the_Accuser_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ronan the Accuser (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), led by [Korath](/wiki/Korath_the_Pursuer_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Korath the Pursuer (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). Although Quill escapes with the Orb, Yondu discovers his theft and issues a [bounty](/wiki/Bounty_(reward) "Bounty (reward)") for his capture, while Ronan sends the assassin [Gamora](/wiki/Gamora_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Gamora (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") after the Orb.

When Quill attempts to sell the Orb on [Xandar](/wiki/Xandar_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Xandar (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), the capital of the Nova Empire, Gamora ambushes him and steals it. A fight ensues, drawing in a pair of bounty hunters: [Rocket](/wiki/Rocket_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Rocket (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), a genetically and cybernetically modified raccoon, and his companion, the tree-like humanoid [Groot](/wiki/Groot_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Groot (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). [Nova Corps](/wiki/Nova_Corps_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Nova Corps (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") officers capture the four, detaining them in the [Kyln](/wiki/Kyln "Kyln") prison. An inmate, [Drax the Destroyer](/wiki/Drax_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Drax (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), attempts to kill Gamora due to her association with Ronan, who killed his wife and daughter. Quill convinces Drax that Gamora can bring Ronan to him, though Gamora reveals that she has betrayed Ronan, unwilling to let him use the Orb's power. Learning that Gamora intends to sell the Orb to the Collector [Taneleer Tivan](/wiki/Taneleer_Tivan_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Taneleer Tivan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), Quill, Rocket, Groot, and Drax work together to escape the Kyln in Quill's ship, the *[Milano](/wiki/Milano_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Milano (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")*.

Ronan meets with Gamora's adoptive father, [Thanos](/wiki/Thanos_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), to discuss her betrayal. Quill's group flees to [Knowhere](/wiki/Knowhere_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Knowhere (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), a city planet inhabited by thieves and merchants made from the severed head of a dead Celestial. A drunken Drax summons Ronan while the rest of the group meets Tivan. Tivan opens the Orb, revealing an [Infinity Stone](/wiki/Infinity_Stone "Infinity Stone"), an item of immeasurable power that destroys all but the most powerful beings who wield it. Tivan's slave, Carina, grabs the Stone, triggering an explosion that engulfs Tivan's collection. Ronan arrives and easily defeats Drax, while the others flee by ship, pursued by Ronan's followers, including Gamora's adoptive sister [Nebula](/wiki/Nebula_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Nebula (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). Nebula destroys Gamora's ship, leaving her floating in space, and Ronan's fighters capture the Orb.

Quill contacts Yondu before following Gamora into space, giving her his helmet to survive; Yondu arrives and retrieves the pair. Rocket, Drax, and Groot threaten to attack Yondu's ship to rescue them, but Quill negotiates a truce, promising the Orb to Yondu. Quill's group agrees that facing Ronan means certain death but that they cannot let him use the Infinity Stone to destroy the galaxy. On Ronan's flagship, the *[Dark Aster](/wiki/Dark_Aster_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Dark Aster (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")*, Ronan embeds the Stone in his warhammer, taking its power for himself. He contacts Thanos, threatening to kill him after first destroying Xandar. Hateful of her adoptive father, Nebula allies with Ronan.

The Ravagers and Quill's group join the Nova Corps to confront the *Dark Aster* at Xandar, with Quill's group breaching the warship with the *Milano*. Ronan uses his empowered warhammer to destroy the Nova Corps fleet. Drax kills Korath, and Gamora defeats Nebula, who escapes. However, the group finds themselves outmatched by Ronan's power. Rocket crashes a Ravager ship through the *Dark Aster*. The damaged *Dark Aster* crash-lands on Xandar, with Groot sacrificing himself to shield the group. Ronan emerges from the wreck and prepares to destroy Xandar, but Quill distracts him, allowing Drax and Rocket to destroy Ronan's warhammer. Quill grabs the freed Stone, and with Gamora, Drax, and Rocket sharing its burden, uses it to vaporize Ronan.

Quill gives Yondu a container supposedly containing the Stone and gives the real one to the Nova Corps. As the Ravagers leave Xandar, [Kraglin Obfonteri](/wiki/Kraglin_Obfonteri "Kraglin Obfonteri") remarks that it turned out well that they did not deliver Quill to his father per their contract. Quill's group, now known as the [Guardians of the Galaxy](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Guardians of the Galaxy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), has their criminal records [expunged](/wiki/Expunged "Expunged"), and Quill learns that he is only half-human, his father being part of an ancient, unknown species. Quill finally opens the last present he received from his mother, a [cassette tape](/wiki/Cassette_tape "Cassette tape") filled with her favorite songs. The Guardians leave in the rebuilt *Milano* along with a potted sapling [cut](/wiki/Cutting_(plant) "Cutting (plant)") from Groot, which grows into a baby version of him.

In a [post-credits scene](/wiki/Post-credits_scene "Post-credits scene"), Tivan sits in his destroyed archive with two of his living exhibits: a canine cosmonaut and an anthropomorphic duck.[[a]](#cite_note-8)

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(film)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6a/Guardians_cast.jpg/250px-Guardians_cast.jpg)](/wiki/File:Guardians_cast.jpg "Use the scrollbar to see the full image.")

The cast of *Guardians of the Galaxy* promoting the film at the 2013 [San Diego Comic-Con](/wiki/San_Diego_Comic-Con "San Diego Comic-Con"). From top to bottom: Pratt, Saldaña, Bautista, Pace, Rooker, Gillan, Hounsou, and del Toro (scrollable image)

* [Chris Pratt](/wiki/Chris_Pratt "Chris Pratt") as [Peter Quill / Star-Lord](/wiki/Peter_Quill_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"):  
  The half-human, half-alien[[8]](#cite_note-SuperheroHypePg1-9) leader of the [Guardians](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Guardians of the Galaxy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who was abducted from [Missouri](/wiki/Missouri "Missouri") as a child in 1988 and raised by a group of alien thieves and smugglers called the [Ravagers](/wiki/Ravagers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ravagers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").[[9]](#cite_note-StitchKingdom-10)[[10]](#cite_note-USATodayFeb2014-11)[[11]](#cite_note-SlashFilmApr2014-12) About the character, Pratt said, "He had a hard time as a kid, and now he goes around space, making out with hot alien girls and just being a rogue and a bit of a jerk, and through teaming up with these guys, finds a higher purpose for himself".[[12]](#cite_note-LATimes-13) He also added that the character is a mix of [Han Solo](/wiki/Han_Solo "Han Solo") and [Marty McFly](/wiki/Marty_McFly "Marty McFly").[[8]](#cite_note-SuperheroHypePg1-9) Pratt, who was mostly known for playing supporting characters, including [Andy Dwyer](/wiki/Andy_Dwyer "Andy Dwyer") on the television series *[Parks and Recreation](/wiki/Parks_and_Recreation "Parks and Recreation")*, initially turned down the role. Pratt had lost weight to portray fit characters in films such as *[Moneyball](/wiki/Moneyball_(film) "Moneyball (film)")* (2011) and *[Zero Dark Thirty](/wiki/Zero_Dark_Thirty "Zero Dark Thirty")* (2012), and had given up ambitions to play the lead role in action films after humbling auditions for *[Star Trek](/wiki/Star_Trek_(2009_film) "Star Trek (2009 film)")* and *[Avatar](/wiki/Avatar_(2009_film) "Avatar (2009 film)")* (both 2009). Casting director Sarah Finn suggested Pratt to Gunn, who dismissed the idea despite struggling to cast that role. Despite this, Finn arranged for a meeting between the two, at which point Gunn was immediately convinced that Pratt was perfect for the role. Pratt also won over Feige, despite having gained weight again for *[Delivery Man](/wiki/Delivery_Man_(film) "Delivery Man (film)")* (2013).[[13]](#cite_note-EWJuly2014-14) Prior to filming, Pratt underwent a strict diet and training regimen to lose 60 pounds (27 kg) in six months.[[14]](#cite_note-PrattWeight-15) Pratt signed a multi-film contract with Marvel,[[15]](#cite_note-HuffingtonPostNov2013-16) and was granted a temporary leave from his work on *Parks and Recreation* to accommodate his participation in the film.[[16]](#cite_note-ParksandRec-17) [Wyatt Oleff](/wiki/Wyatt_Oleff "Wyatt Oleff") portrays a young Quill.[[17]](#cite_note-TimeReview-18)
* [Zoe Saldaña](/wiki/Zoe_Salda%C3%B1a "Zoe Saldaña") as [Gamora](/wiki/Gamora_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Gamora (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"):  
  An orphan from an alien world who seeks redemption for her past crimes. She was trained by Thanos to be his personal [assassin](/wiki/Assassin "Assassin").[[9]](#cite_note-StitchKingdom-10)[[18]](#cite_note-AICN-19)[[19]](#cite_note-Empire-20) Saldaña said that she became Gamora through make-up rather than [computer-generated imagery](/wiki/Computer-generated_imagery "Computer-generated imagery") (CGI) or [performance capture](/wiki/Motion_capture "Motion capture").[[20]](#cite_note-2013MaySaldaña-21) On taking the role, Saldaña said, "I was just excited to be asked to join by James Gunn and to also play someone green. I've been blue before [in *Avatar*]".[[21]](#cite_note-2013MayMTV-22) Saldaña described Gamora as "... a warrior, she's an assassin and she's very lethal, but what saves her is the same thing that can doom her. She has a sense of righteousness. She's a very righteous individual".[[8]](#cite_note-SuperheroHypePg1-9)
* [Dave Bautista](/wiki/Dave_Bautista "Dave Bautista") as [Drax the Destroyer](/wiki/Drax_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Drax (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"):  
  A warrior who seeks to avenge his family's death at the hands of Ronan.[[18]](#cite_note-AICN-19) On relating to the character, Bautista said, "I can just relate to Drax so much it's not even funny. Just the simple things that we have in common. Simple things like the tattoos, the tragedy—because, you know, I had a bit of tragedy in my life, as well. So it's really easy for me to pull from that". Bautista also said that there was "a lot of comic relief to Drax", but the character was not aware of it. Bautista stated that he did not do much preparation for the role, because "Luckily, for me, I'm a lifelong athlete and I adapted real quick".[[22]](#cite_note-2014Bautista-23) Bautista's makeup took approximately four hours to apply, though it could be removed in just 90 minutes.[[23]](#cite_note-ColliderMay2014-24) Drax has various scarring patterns on his body, which replace the simple tattoos from the comics, each having a specific story. Additionally, his skin tone was changed from the bright green in the comics to a muddier gray, to avoid visual similarities to the [Hulk](/wiki/Hulk "Hulk").[[24]](#cite_note-CBRSetVisit-25)
* [Vin Diesel](/wiki/Vin_Diesel "Vin Diesel") as [Groot](/wiki/Groot_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Groot (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"):  
  A tree-like humanoid,[[25]](#cite_note-MarvelSynopsis-26) he is the accomplice of Rocket. Diesel stated that he provided the voice and [motion capture](/wiki/Motion_capture "Motion capture") for Groot, after originally being in talks to star in a new [Phase Three](/wiki/Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_Phase_Three "Marvel Cinematic Universe: Phase Three") Marvel film.[[26]](#cite_note-DieselTHR-27)[[27]](#cite_note-DieselDeadline-28)[[28]](#cite_note-DieselConfirm-29) Diesel also provided Groot's voice for several foreign-language releases of the film.[[29]](#cite_note-DieselForeignVoices-30) Krystian Godlewski portrayed the character on set, though his acting was not used in the final character CGI.[[30]](#cite_note-SeanGunnThanos-31)[[31]](#cite_note-RocketGrootBI-32) On the character, which Gunn based on his dog,[[32]](#cite_note-RocketGrootInfluence-33) Gunn said, "All the Guardians start out the movie as bastards—except Groot. He's an innocent. He's a hundred percent deadly and a hundred percent sweet. He's caught up in Rocket's life, really". Gunn added that the design and movement of Groot took "the better part of a year" to create.[[33]](#cite_note-TotalFilmTrailer3-34) Gunn added, "The ways in which Vin Diesel says, 'I am Groot,' I am astounded. All of the 'I am Groots' that were earlier voices didn't sound very good at all ... Vin came in and in one day, laid down all these 'I am Groot' tracks, and he's a perfectionist. He made me explain to him with ever [*[sic](/wiki/Sic "Sic")*] 'I am Groot,' exactly what he was saying ... It was amazing when we first put that voice in there how much the character changed and how much he influenced the character".[[34]](#cite_note-GunnIGNFeb2014-35) Regarding the limited words used by Groot, Diesel said in many ways this was, "... the most challenging thing to ask an actor to do".[[35]](#cite_note-TheMovieNetwork-36) Diesel found an emotional note in his performance, invoking the death of his friend and *[Fast & Furious](/wiki/Fast_%26_Furious "Fast & Furious")* co-star [Paul Walker](/wiki/Paul_Walker "Paul Walker"), saying, "This was in December [2013], and the first time I came back to dealing with human beings after dealing with death, so playing a character who celebrates life in the way Groot does was very nice".[[36]](#cite_note-DieselGrootDeath-37) Groot's form and size-changing abilities are seen, with Gunn stating that he has the ability to grow in the film.[[37]](#cite_note-SuperheroHypePg2-38)
* [Bradley Cooper](/wiki/Bradley_Cooper "Bradley Cooper") as [Rocket](/wiki/Rocket_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Rocket (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"):  
  A genetically-engineered [raccoon](/wiki/Raccoon "Raccoon")-based bounty hunter, mercenary, and master of weapons and battle tactics.[[9]](#cite_note-StitchKingdom-10)[[25]](#cite_note-MarvelSynopsis-26)[[38]](#cite_note-CooperOfficial-39)[[39]](#cite_note-Cooper-40) Gunn worked with live raccoons to get the correct feel for the character, and to make sure it was "not a cartoon character", saying, "It's not [Bugs Bunny](/wiki/Bugs_Bunny "Bugs Bunny") in the middle of the [Avengers](/wiki/Avengers_(comics) "Avengers (comics)"), it's a real, little, somewhat mangled beast that's alone. There's no one else in the universe quite like him, he's been created by these guys to be a mean-ass fighting machine".[[40]](#cite_note-GunnRocket-41) Out of his original feelings that a talking raccoon could look a bit goofy in a superhero film, Gunn came up with a tragic [origin story](/wiki/Origin_story "Origin story") for Rocket that made him the "saddest creature" in the universe; said backstory would be eventually explored in *[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._3 "Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3")* (2023).[[41]](#cite_note-RocketsOriginsVol3PlannedHere-42) Gunn also based the character on himself.[[32]](#cite_note-RocketGrootInfluence-33) Describing Rocket in relation to the rest of the Guardians, Cooper said, "I think Rocket is dynamic. He's the sort of [Joe Pesci](/wiki/Joe_Pesci "Joe Pesci") in *[Goodfellas](/wiki/Goodfellas "Goodfellas")* (1990) guy".[[42]](#cite_note-Cooper2-43) Cooper voiced Rocket, while [Sean Gunn](/wiki/Sean_Gunn "Sean Gunn") (James' younger brother) stood in for the character during filming.[[43]](#cite_note-RocketStandIn-44) James Gunn said that for the role of Rocket, some physical movements from Cooper, including facial expressions and hand movements, were recorded as potential references for the animators,[[44]](#cite_note-2013NovDelToro-45) though much of Sean Gunn's acting is used throughout the film.[[30]](#cite_note-SeanGunnThanos-31) Sean noted they "kind of stumbled" into the process of him performing on set since they "weren't sure how we were going to create that character". The same process continued to be used for all subsequent appearances of Rocket.[[45]](#cite_note-SeanGunnSept2018-46) Before Cooper was cast, James Gunn said that it was a challenge finding a voice for Rocket, that he was looking for someone who could balance "the fast-talking speech patterns that Rocket has, but also can be funny, because he is really funny. But also has the heart that Rocket has. Because there are actually some pretty dramatic scenes with Rocket".[[46]](#cite_note-IGNRocketJun2014-47)
* [Lee Pace](/wiki/Lee_Pace "Lee Pace") as [Ronan the Accuser](/wiki/Ronan_the_Accuser_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ronan the Accuser (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"):  
  A [Kree](/wiki/Kree "Kree") zealot and war criminal who agrees to retrieve an artifact for Thanos in exchange for the annihilation of his mortal enemies, the Xandarians. Ronan and his [Sakaaran](/wiki/Sakaar_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Sakaar (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") army[[47]](#cite_note-SuperheroHypePg3-48) hunt down the Guardians when they interfere with his goals.[[9]](#cite_note-StitchKingdom-10)[[48]](#cite_note-AMCYoutube-49) Describing Ronan, Gunn said, "He is the primary villain, and he is a really twisted guy, he has a really religious bent in this film. He has a very sick and twisted view of what morality is; strength is virtue and weakness is sin and that is what he lives by, and I think he is very scary because of his beliefs, which are real to him".[[49]](#cite_note-DigitalSpy-50) Pace, who originally auditioned for Peter Quill,[[50]](#cite_note-2012DecPace-51) described Ronan as a "psycho" and a "monster".[[51]](#cite_note-PaceOctober2013-52)
* [Michael Rooker](/wiki/Michael_Rooker "Michael Rooker") as [Yondu Udonta](/wiki/Yondu_Udonta_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Yondu Udonta (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"):  
  A blue-skinned bandit who is the leader of the Ravagers and a paternal figure to Quill.[[9]](#cite_note-StitchKingdom-10)[[52]](#cite_note-2013AprilRooker-53) Yondu helps Quill to steal the orb before Quill betrays him, leaving Yondu and the Ravagers to chase the Guardians. On the character, Rooker said, he has "some interesting issues—not a good guy, not a bad guy. There's hope and there's a heart inside Yondu". Gunn created the film's version of the character specifically with Rooker in mind, while borrowing the character's mohawk and use of a whistle-controlled arrow from the comics. Rooker fully committed to the role once he knew his role on the TV series *[The Walking Dead](/wiki/The_Walking_Dead_(TV_series) "The Walking Dead (TV series)")* would be ending.[[53]](#cite_note-RookerUSAToday-54) Rooker's makeup took approximately four hours to apply.[[54]](#cite_note-RookerMakeup-55)
* [Karen Gillan](/wiki/Karen_Gillan "Karen Gillan") as [Nebula](/wiki/Nebula_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Nebula (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"):  
  An adopted daughter of Thanos who was raised with Gamora as siblings and is a loyal lieutenant in the employ of Ronan and Thanos.[[9]](#cite_note-StitchKingdom-10)[[11]](#cite_note-SlashFilmApr2014-12) About the character, Gillan said, "She is the female villain of the film ... She is very sadistic and evil, but I like to think for a very valid reason".[[55]](#cite_note-2013NovGillan-56) She also added, "I think she's a really interesting character. What I like to play around with is how jealous she is. She's Gamora's sister, and there's a lot of [sibling rivalry](/wiki/Sibling_rivalry "Sibling rivalry"). That's the most interesting aspect to me, because jealousy can consume you and turn you bitter, and ugly. And she's a total sadist, so that's fun too".[[56]](#cite_note-GillanCBR-57) Gunn and Gillan worked to create Nebula's voice as a simultaneous impression of [Marilyn Monroe](/wiki/Marilyn_Monroe "Marilyn Monroe") and [Clint Eastwood](/wiki/Clint_Eastwood "Clint Eastwood").[[57]](#cite_note-GunnApril2023RollingStone-58) Gillan researched the ancient [Spartans](/wiki/Sparta "Sparta"), shaved off her hair and trained for two months for the role.[[56]](#cite_note-GillanCBR-57)[[58]](#cite_note-2013JulyComicCon-59) The character's makeup took approximately four-and-a-half hours to be applied.[[56]](#cite_note-GillanCBR-57)
* [Djimon Hounsou](/wiki/Djimon_Hounsou "Djimon Hounsou") as [Korath](/wiki/Korath_the_Pursuer_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Korath the Pursuer (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"):  
  A Kree[[47]](#cite_note-SuperheroHypePg3-48) ally of Ronan who is a feared intergalactic hunter.[[9]](#cite_note-StitchKingdom-10) As to why he took the role, Hounsou said, "I have a four-year old son who loves superheroes from [Spider-Man](/wiki/Spider-Man "Spider-Man") to [Iron Man](/wiki/Iron_Man "Iron Man") to [Batman](/wiki/Batman "Batman"). He's got all the costumes. One day he looks at me and says 'Dad, I want to be light-skinned so I could be Spider-Man. Spider-Man has light skin.' That was sort of a shock. This is why I am excited to be a part of the Marvel Universe, so I could hopefully provide that diversity in the role of the superhero".[[59]](#cite_note-2013JulyHounsou-60)
* [John C. Reilly](/wiki/John_C._Reilly "John C. Reilly") as [Rhomann Dey](/wiki/Rhomann_Dey_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Rhomann Dey (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"): A corpsman in the [Nova Corps](/wiki/Nova_Corps "Nova Corps"), the Nova Empire's military and police force.[[9]](#cite_note-StitchKingdom-10)[[60]](#cite_note-2013JuneReilly-61)
* [Glenn Close](/wiki/Glenn_Close "Glenn Close") as [Irani Rael](/wiki/Irani_Rael_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Irani Rael (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"):  
  The leader of the Nova Corps, known as Nova Prime, whose mission is to protect the citizens of the Nova Empire and keep peace.[[9]](#cite_note-StitchKingdom-10)[[61]](#cite_note-2013JulyMarvel-62)[[62]](#cite_note-2013MayClose-63) Close stated that she "had always wanted to be in a movie like this", and that it would be "the most fun to play something like the [Judi Dench](/wiki/Judi_Dench "Judi Dench") [as [M](/wiki/M_(James_Bond) "M (James Bond)")] or [Samuel L. Jackson](/wiki/Samuel_L._Jackson "Samuel L. Jackson") [as [Nick Fury](/wiki/Nick_Fury_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")] role".[[63]](#cite_note-VultureClose-64) She said that she took the role because she "love[s] to do stuff that's different" and wanted to show that she "[has] always been up for anything".[[64]](#cite_note-USAClose-65) She also stated that her contract has "several" films on it, and that she would be open to working on other Marvel Studios films in addition to returning for *Guardians* sequels.[[63]](#cite_note-VultureClose-64)
* [Benicio del Toro](/wiki/Benicio_del_Toro "Benicio del Toro") as [Taneleer Tivan / The Collector](/wiki/Taneleer_Tivan_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Taneleer Tivan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"):  
  An obsessive keeper of the largest collection of interstellar fauna, relics, and species in the galaxy who operates out of a place in space named [Knowhere](/wiki/Knowhere "Knowhere").[[9]](#cite_note-StitchKingdom-10)[[61]](#cite_note-2013JulyMarvel-62)[[65]](#cite_note-2013MayDelToro-66) Describing del Toro's performance, Gunn said, "He's like an outer-space [Liberace](/wiki/Liberace "Liberace"). That's what it says in the script, which he's kind of doing".[[44]](#cite_note-2013NovDelToro-45) On bringing the character to life, del Toro said, "What James [Gunn] wanted, that I found out little by little as I was doing it, is that he wanted me to explore and just keep pushing the character and keep creating [him] as I was in front of the camera".[[66]](#cite_note-2014JulDelToro-67)

Additionally, [Josh Brolin](/wiki/Josh_Brolin "Josh Brolin") appears, uncredited, as [Thanos](/wiki/Thanos_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") through voice acting and performance capture, taking over the role from [Damion Poitier](/wiki/Damion_Poitier "Damion Poitier").[[67]](#cite_note-ThanosCast-68)[[68]](#cite_note-BrolinThanos-69)[[69]](#cite_note-BrolinPerformance-70) [Sean Gunn](/wiki/Sean_Gunn "Sean Gunn") stood in for Thanos during filming and portrays [Kraglin Obfonteri](/wiki/Kraglin_Obfonteri "Kraglin Obfonteri"), Yondu's first mate in the Ravagers.[[30]](#cite_note-SeanGunnThanos-31) [Alexis Denisof](/wiki/Alexis_Denisof "Alexis Denisof") reprises his role as Thanos' [vizier](/wiki/Vizier "Vizier"), "[the Other](/wiki/The_Other_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "The Other (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")", from *[The Avengers](/wiki/The_Avengers_(2012_film) "The Avengers (2012 film)")* (2012).[[70]](#cite_note-Denisof-71) [Ophelia Lovibond](/wiki/Ophelia_Lovibond "Ophelia Lovibond") plays [Carina](/wiki/Carina_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Carina (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), the Collector's slave;[[71]](#cite_note-2013AprilLovibond-72)[[72]](#cite_note-2014AprilLovibond-73) [Peter officer;[[73]](#cite_note-Serafinowicz-74)[[74]](#cite_note-ComingSoonReview-75) [Gregg Henry](/wiki/Gregg_Henry "Gregg Henry") plays Quill's grandfather;[[75]](#cite_note-Henry-76) [Laura Universe)");[[74]](#cite_note-ComingSoonReview-75)[[76]](#cite_note-Haddock-77) [Melia Kreiling](/wiki/Melia_Kreiling "Melia Kreiling") plays [Bereet](/wiki/Bereet_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Bereet (Marvel Cinematic Universe)");[[77]](#cite_note-Kreiling-78) [Christopher Fairbank](/wiki/Christopher_Fairbank "Christopher Fairbank") plays The Broker;[[78]](#cite_note-Fairbank-79) [Mikaela
